heading: Interest on certain home mortgages
label: 25 Interest on certain home mortgages
notes: Amendment of Subsection (e)(1)(C), (c)(1), , , 218, provided that, applicable to taxable years ending after , subsection (e)(1)(C) of this section is amended by striking “and 25D” and inserting “25D, and 25F”. children.label: A
children.text: the certificate credit rate, and

children.label: B
children.text: the interest paid or accrued by the taxpayer during the taxable year on the remaining principal of the certified indebtedness amount.

children.heading: Limitation where credit rate exceeds 20 percent
children.label: 2 Limitation where credit rate exceeds 20 percent

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the certificate credit rate exceeds 20 percent, the amount of the credit allowed to the taxpayer under paragraph (1) for any taxable year shall not exceed $2,000.

children.heading: Special rule where 2 or more persons hold interests in residence
children.label: B Special rule where 2 or more persons hold interests in residence
children.text: If 2 or more persons hold interests in any residence, the limitation of subparagraph (A) shall be allocated among such persons in proportion to their respective interests in the residence.

children.heading: Certificate credit rate; certified indebtedness amount
children.label: b Certificate credit rate; certified indebtedness amount

children.heading: Certificate credit rate
children.label: 1 Certificate credit rate
children.text: The term “certificate credit rate” means the rate of the credit allowable by this section which is specified in the mortgage credit certificate.

children.label: i
children.text: to acquire the principal residence of the taxpayer,

children.label: ii
children.text: as a qualified home improvement loan (as defined in section 143(k)(4)) with respect to such residence, or

children.label: iii
children.text: as a qualified rehabilitation loan (as defined in section 143(k)(5)) with respect to such residence, and

children.label: B
children.text: specified in the mortgage credit certificate.

children.heading: Mortgage credit certificate; qualified mortgage credit certificate program
children.label: c Mortgage credit certificate; qualified mortgage credit certificate program
A
children.text: is issued under a qualified mortgage credit certificate program by the State or political subdivision having the authority to issue a qualified mortgage bond to provide financing on the principal residence of the taxpayer,

children.label: B
children.text: is issued to the taxpayer in connection with the acquisition, qualified rehabilitation, or qualified home improvement of the taxpayer’s principal residence,

children.label: i
children.text: the certificate credit rate, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the certified indebtedness amount, and

children.label: D
children.text: is in such form as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.label: i
children.text: which is established by a State or political subdivision thereof for any calendar year for which it is authorized to issue qualified mortgage bonds,

children.label: ii
children.text: under which the issuing authority elects (in such manner and form as the Secretary may prescribe) not to issue an amount of private activity bonds which it may otherwise issue during such calendar year under section 146,

children.label: I
children.text: subsection (c) (relating to residence requirements),

children.label: II
children.text: subsection (d) (relating to 3-year requirement),

children.label: III
children.text: subsection (e) (relating to purchase price requirement),

children.label: IV
children.text: subsection (f) (relating to income requirements),

children.label: V
children.text: subsection (h) (relating to portion of loans required to be placed in targeted areas), and

children.label: VI
children.text: paragraph (1) of subsection (i) (relating to other requirements),

children.label: iv
children.text: under which no mortgage credit certificate may be issued with respect to any residence any of the financing of which is provided from the proceeds of a qualified mortgage bond or a qualified veterans’ mortgage bond,

children.label: v
children.text: except to the extent provided in regulations, which is not limited to indebtedness incurred from particular lenders,

children.label: vi
children.text: except to the extent provided in regulations, which provides that a mortgage credit certificate is not transferrable, and

children.label: vii
children.text: if the issuing authority allocates a block of mortgage credit certificates for use in connection with a particular development, which requires the developer to furnish to the issuing authority and the homebuyer a certificate that the price for the residence is no higher than it would be without the use of a mortgage credit certificate.

children.label: i
children.text: each qualified mortgage certificate credit program shall be treated as a separate issue,

children.label: I
children.text: the certified indebtedness amount of each mortgage credit certificate issued under such program, by

children.label: II
children.text: the certificate credit rate specified in such certificate,

children.label: iii
children.text: paragraph (1) of section 143(d) shall be applied by substituting “100 percent” for “95 percent or more”.

children.heading: Determination of certificate credit rate
children.label: d Determination of certificate credit rate

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The certificate credit rate specified in any mortgage credit certificate shall not be less than 10 percent or more than 50 percent.

children.heading: Aggregate limit on certificate credit rates
children.label: 2 Aggregate limit on certificate credit rates
i
children.text: the certified indebtedness amount of each mortgage credit certificate issued under such program, by

children.label: ii
children.text: the certificate credit rate with respect to such certificate,

children.heading: Nonissued bond amount
children.label: B Nonissued bond amount
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “nonissued bond amount” means, with respect to any qualified mortgage credit certificate program, the amount of qualified mortgage bonds which the issuing authority is otherwise authorized to issue and elects not to issue under subsection (c)(2)(A)(ii).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the credit allowable under subsection (a) for any taxable year exceeds the applicable tax limit for such taxable year, such excess shall be a carryover to each of the 3 succeeding taxable years and, subject to the limitations of subparagraph (B), shall be added to the credit allowable by subsection (a) for such succeeding taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: the credit allowable under subsection (a) for such taxable year determined without regard to this paragraph, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amounts which, by reason of this paragraph, are carried to such taxable year and are attributable to taxable years before the unused credit year.

children.heading: Applicable tax limit
children.label: C Applicable tax limit
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “applicable tax limit” means the limitation imposed by section 26(a) for the taxable year reduced by the sum of the credits allowable under this subpart (other than this section and sections 23 and 25D).

children.heading: Indebtedness not treated as certified where certain requirements not in fact met
children.label: 2 Indebtedness not treated as certified where certain requirements not in fact met
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to any indebtedness if all the requirements of subsection (c)(1), (d), (e), (f), and (i) of section 143 and clauses (iv), (v), and (vii) of subsection (c)(2)(A), were not in fact met with respect to such indebtedness. Except to the extent provided in regulations, the requirements described in the preceding sentence shall be treated as met if there is a certification, under penalty of perjury, that such requirements are met.

children.heading: Period for which certificate in effect
children.label: 3 Period for which certificate in effect
i
children.text: beginning on the date such certificate is issued, and

children.label: I
children.text: the certificate is revoked by the issuing authority, or

children.label: II
children.text: the residence to which such certificate relates ceases to be the principal residence of the individual to whom the certificate relates.

children.heading: Certificate invalid unless indebtedness incurred within certain period
children.label: B Certificate invalid unless indebtedness incurred within certain period
children.text: A certificate shall not apply to any indebtedness which is incurred after the close of the second calendar year following the calendar year for which the issuing authority made the applicable election under subsection (c)(2)(A)(ii).

children.heading: Notice to Secretary when certificate revoked
children.label: C Notice to Secretary when certificate revoked
children.text: Any issuing authority which revokes any mortgage credit certificate shall notify the Secretary of such revocation at such time and in such manner as the Secretary shall prescribe by regulations.

children.heading: Reissuance of mortgage credit certificates
children.label: 4 Reissuance of mortgage credit certificates
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe regulations which allow the administrator of a mortgage credit certificate program to reissue a mortgage credit certificate specifying a certified mortgage indebtedness that replaces the outstanding balance of the certified mortgage indebtedness specified on the original certificate to any taxpayer to whom the original certificate was issued, under such terms and conditions as the Secretary determines are necessary to ensure that the amount of the credit allowable under subsection (a) with respect to such reissued certificate is equal to or less than the amount of credit which would be allowable under subsection (a) with respect to the original certificate for any taxable year ending after such reissuance.

children.heading: Public notice that certificates will be issued
children.label: 5 Public notice that certificates will be issued
A
children.text: the eligibility requirements for such certificate,

children.label: B
children.text: the methods by which such certificates are to be issued, and

children.label: C
children.text: such other information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Interest paid or accrued to related persons
children.label: 6 Interest paid or accrued to related persons
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under subsection (a) for any interest paid or accrued to a person who is a related person to the taxpayer (within the meaning of section 144(a)(3)(A)).

children.heading: Principal residence
children.label: 7 Principal residence
children.text: The term “principal residence” has the same meaning as when used in section 121.

children.heading: Qualified rehabilitation
children.label: A Qualified rehabilitation
children.text: The term “qualified rehabilitation” has the meaning given such term by section 143(k)(5)(B).

children.heading: Qualified home improvement
children.label: B Qualified home improvement
children.text: The term “qualified home improvement” means an alteration, repair, or improvement described in section 143(k)(4).

children.heading: Qualified mortgage bond
children.label: 9 Qualified mortgage bond
children.text: The term “qualified mortgage bond” has the meaning given such term by section 143(a)(1).

children.heading: Manufactured housing
children.label: 10 Manufactured housing
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “single family residence” includes any manufactured home which has a minimum of 400 square feet of living space and a minimum width in excess of 102 inches and which is of a kind customarily used at a fixed location. Nothing in the preceding sentence shall be construed as providing that such a home will be taken into account in making determinations under section 143.

children.heading: Reduction in aggregate amount of qualified mortgage bonds which may be issued where certain requirements not met
children.label: f Reduction in aggregate amount of qualified mortgage bonds which may be issued where certain requirements not met

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If for any calendar year any mortgage credit certificate program which satisfies procedural requirements with respect to volume limitations prescribed by the Secretary fails to meet the requirements of paragraph (2) of subsection (d), such requirements shall be treated as satisfied with respect to any certified indebtedness of such program, but the applicable State ceiling under subsection (d) of section 146 for the State in which such program operates shall be reduced by 1.25 times the correction amount with respect to such failure. Such reduction shall be applied to such State ceiling for the calendar year following the calendar year in which the Secretary determines the correction amount with respect to such failure.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the term “correction amount” means an amount equal to the excess credit amount divided by 0.25.

children.label: I
children.text: the credit amount for any mortgage credit certificate program, over

children.label: II
children.text: the amount which would have been the credit amount for such program had such program met the requirements of paragraph (2) of subsection (d).

children.heading: Credit amount
children.label: ii Credit amount
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the term “credit amount” means the sum of the products determined under clauses (i) and (ii) of subsection (d)(2)(A).

children.heading: Special rule for States having constitutional home rule cities
children.label: 3 Special rule for States having constitutional home rule cities
children.text: In the case of a State having one or more constitutional home rule cities (within the meaning of section 146(d)(3)(C)), the reduction in the State ceiling by reason of paragraph (1) shall be allocated to the constitutional home rule city, or to the portion of the State not within such city, whichever caused the reduction.

children.heading: Exception where certification program
children.label: 4 Exception where certification program
children.text: The provisions of this subsection shall not apply in any case in which there is a certification program which is designed to ensure that the requirements of this section are met and which meets such requirements as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 5 Waiver
children.text: The Secretary may waive the application of paragraph (1) in any case in which he determines that the failure is due to reasonable cause.
the name, address, and social security account number of the individual to which the certificate was issued,
the certificate’s issuer, date of issue, certified indebtedness amount, and certificate credit rate, and
such other information as the Secretary may require by regulations.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 1 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this section, including regulations which may require recipients of mortgage credit certificates to pay a reasonable processing fee to defray the expenses incurred in administering the program.

children.heading: Contracts
children.label: 2 Contracts
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to enter into contracts with any person to provide services in connection with the administration of this section.

children.heading: Recapture of portion of Federal subsidy from use of mortgage credit certificates
children.label: i Recapture of portion of Federal subsidy from use of mortgage credit certificates
children.text: For provisions increasing the tax imposed by this chapter to recapture a portion of the Federal subsidy from the use of mortgage credit certificates, see section 143(m).
